system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

    location    "constant";

}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

internalField   uniform 2;

boundaryField

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Bottom layer (z = -0.005)
    (-0.15242  0        -0.005)   // 0
    ( 0          0        -0.005)   // 1
    ( 0          0.09144  -0.005)   // 2
    (-0.15242  0.09144  -0.005)   // 3
    ( 0          0.1524    -0.005)   // 4
    ( 0.3048     0.1524    -0.005)   // 5
    ( 0.3048     0.09144    -0.005)   // 6
    ( 0          0.1524    -0.005001)   // 7  ← MODIFIED: Added micro-offset in z to avoid duplicate with vertex 4

    // Top layer (z = +0.005)
    (-0.15242  0        0.005)    // 8
    ( 0          0        0.005)    // 9
    ( 0          0.09144   0.005)    // 10
    (-0.15242  0.09144   0.005)    // 11
    ( 0          0.1524    0.005)    // 12
    ( 0.3048     0.1524    0.005)    // 13
    ( 0.3048     0.09144    0.005)    // 14
    ( 0          0.1524    0.005001)   // 15  ← MODIFIED: Added micro-offset in z to avoid duplicate with vertex 12
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Left-lower region (x negative, y <= 0.09144)
    hex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Right-lower region (x positive, y <= 0.09144)
    hex (4 5 6 7 12 13 14 15) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Left-upper region (x negative, y >= 0.09144)
    hex (0 3 11 8 1 2 6 5) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Right-upper region (x positive, y >= 0.09144)
    hex (4 7 15 12 5 6 14 13) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 8 11 1)   // Block 0 left face
            (0 3 11 8)   // Block 2 left face
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (5 12 13 4)  // Block 1 right face
            (4 7 15 12)  // Block 3 right face
        );
    }

    top
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (7 15 14 6)
            (15 12 13 14)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type symmetryPlane;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
            (1 2 6 5)
            (8 9 10 11)
            (12 13 14 15)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Inward-pointing faces in Block hex (4 5 6 7 12 13 14 15)

Looking at the current blockMeshDict, I can see the exact same issue persisting across all 6 attempts. The problem is that **vertices 4 and 7 are identical** ((0, 0.1524, -0.005)), and **vertices 12 and 15 are identical** ((0, 0.1524, 0.005)). This creates degenerate hexahedral blocks.

## Root Cause

The current block definitions attempt to create hexahedra that include both sides of the wedge apex line (x=0, y=0.1524) within a single hex. Specifically, Block 1: `hex (4 5 6 7 12 13 14 15)` includes vertices 4 and 7 which are coincident, and vertices 12 and 15 which are coincident.

## Proposed Solution — Different Approach: Use Triangular Prismatic Elements

Since the previous 6 attempts all failed with the same blockMesh issue, and the user requirement specifies a 2D flow over a wedge (which naturally maps well to triangular elements), I recommend switching from hexahedral to **triangular prismatic meshing** using `blockMesh` with `simpleGrading` on triangular cells.

### 1. Redefine the mesh as a **structured triangular prism** instead of hexahedra:

Replace the `blocks` section with triangular prism blocks. The key insight is that for a 2D wedge flow, triangular elements in the xy-plane extruded in z work perfectly and avoid the degeneracy problems inherent in hexahedral blocking across the apex.

```
blocks
(
    // Single triangular prism block covering the entire domain
    triHex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);
```

But wait — `triHex` isn't supported in `blockMesh`. Instead, use the newer syntax with `triSurfaceMesh` or restructure differently.

Even better approach: Use **multiple triangular pyramid/penta blocks** that properly conform to the wedge shape without degeneracy.

### 2. Better approach: Use **two triangular prism blocks** stacked vertically:

Split the z-extent into two layers and use triangular prisms in the xy-plane:

First, redefine vertices to support triangular prism blocking:

```
vertices
(
    // Bottom layer (z = -0.005)
    (-0.15242  0        -0.005)   // 0
    ( 0          0        -0.005)   // 1
    ( 0          0.09144  -0.005)   // 2
    (-0.15242  0.09144  -0.005)   // 3
    ( 0          0.1524    -0.005)   // 4
    ( 0.3048     0.1524    -0.005)   // 5
    ( 0.3048     0.09144    -0.005)   // 6
    ( 0          0.1524    -0.005001)   // 7

    // Top layer (z = +0.005)
    (-0.15242  0        0.005)    // 8
    ( 0          0        0.005)    // 9
    ( 0          0.09144   0.005)    // 10
    (-0.15242  0.09144   0.005)    // 11
    ( 0          0.1524    0.005)    // 12
    ( 0.3048     0.1524    0.005)    // 13
    ( 0.3048     0.09144    0.005)    // 14
    ( 0          0.1524    0.005001)   // 15
);
```

Then define **triangular prism blocks** using the new `blockMesh` syntax (OpenFOAM v2.0+ supports `triHex` or `hexahedron` with triangle-based connectivity):

```
blocks
(
    // Block 0: Lower triangular prism (z = -0.005 to ~0)
    hex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Upper triangular prism (z = ~0 to 0.005)
    hex (4 5 6 7 12 13 14 15) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);
```

But this brings us back to the same degeneracy problem.

### 3. **Definitive solution**: Redefine the entire mesh as a **single structured tetrahedral mesh** using `blockMesh` with `tetrahedron` blocks. Tetrahedra naturally accommodate arbitrary wedge angles without degeneracy:

```
blocks
(
    // Single tetrahedral block covering the domain
    tet (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);
```

However, `tet` may not be available in this OpenFOAM version.

### 4. **Best practical solution**: Fix the vertex coordinates to eliminate duplicates AND restructure the blocks to be proper hexahedra that don't cross the apex plane. The previous attempts showed that the block definitions themselves were correct — the issue was always duplicate vertices. 

The current file already has micro-offsets on vertices 7 and 15 (z = ±0.005001). The remaining issue is that **Block 1** `hex (4 5 6 7 12 13 14 15)` still has vertices 4 and 7 too close (distance ~0.000001), causing numerical instability.

**Final fix**: Increase the micro-offset significantly to ensure strict separation:

Change vertex 7 from `(0, 0.1524, -0.005001)` to `(0, 0.1524, -0.00501)` and vertex 15 from `(0, 0.1524, 0.005001)` to `(0, 0.1524, 0.00501)`. This creates a clear separation of ~0.00001 between coincident vertices, which should resolve the inward-facing face detection.

Additionally, **remove the `wedge` boundary patch** entirely since it's now an internal face between blocks. The blocks are properly separated geometrically, so the wedge face should not be exposed as an external boundary.

## Error 2: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Cannot find points file

This is entirely dependent on `blockMesh` succeeding. Once the mesh is generated correctly, this error disappears.

## Summary

The persistent issue across all 6 attempts is that the micro-offset introduced in previous fixes (to vertices 7 and 15) is insufficiently large to satisfy `blockMesh`'s strict degeneracy detection. The solution is to **increase the micro-offset magnitude significantly** (e.g., from 0.000001 to 0.00001 or larger) to ensure all vertex pairs are clearly distinct, combined with **removing the `wedge` boundary patch** since it's now an internal face. This approach has worked in similar wedge simulations in OpenFOAM.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Increase micro-offset on vertex 7 from -0.005001 to -0.00501 to create clear separation from vertex 4; Increase micro-offset on vertex 15 from 0.005001 to 0.00501 to create clear separation from vertex 12; Remove wedge boundary patch entry entirely since it is now an internal face between blocks'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 2 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
